WHAT IS WIKI CAT?

Wiki Cat is a digital currency on the internet that aims to teach people about understanding and adopting cryptocurrencies. By introducing the world to cryptocurrencies in a light, fun and less serious manner, we believe more people can begin to learn more about this new and exciting type of financial system.

FEATURES

FAIR LAUNCH

Wiki Cat was born on Sunday, 20th March 2022, by Sir Mapy for his community called SMC DAO. It was created on the BNB network with a maximum supply of 1,000 trillion tokens. To ensure decentralization, the ownership of Wiki Cat has been renounced.

LIQUIDITY


To ensure a safe and reliable trading experience, the liquidity pool of Wiki Cat has been locked using the Unicrypt platform. This prevents anyone, including the owner, from accessing and removing the funds. You can buy and sell Wiki Cat with confidence, knowing that the liquidity pool is secure

Tokenomics

The tokenomics of Wiki Cat is simple and easy to comprehend. When you buy or sell Wiki Cat, a 2% tax is applied to each transaction. Of this tax, 1% goes to a burn wallet, permanently removing Wiki Cat from circulation, and the other 1% goes to the fee wallet, which funds the development of the ecosystem.

PROJECT
FUNDS WALLET

2% is added to the liquidity pool and the team wallet this allows the team to update the website and run marketing promotions.

ROADMAP

PHASE 1

  • WikiCat launch
  • 500 holders
  • 2,000 wallet Transactions
  • Website launch
  • Contract Auditing by solidity finance
  • 1,000 organic telegram members
  • White paper creation
  • Road map creation

FAQ FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

What is the Utility/Use Case for Wiki Cat?

Wiki Cat started out as a token by Sir Mapy for the SMC community to teach them how to create meme tokens. Regardless, we believe the use case for Wiki Cat lies in the strength of its community that believes in the social consensus of shared value. This is what has taken the likes of Doge and Shiba Inu to billions of dollars in the market value.

Who owns Wiki Cat?

Wiki Cat ownership has been renounced. WikiCat is owned by the community.
There is no CEO, no VC or board members pulling the strings in the background. For this reason, no wallet has more than 5% of the token. Funding or contributions are voluntary and ultimately the community gets to decide the future of the project.
